candidates.name: Email Response Likelihood Forecaster
candidates.pitch: Analyzes email thread characteristics (length, subject, time sent) to score reply probability. Works with Gmail API. Freemium model with daily limit.
candidates.pain: Sales reps waste time following up on emails that were never going to get replies in the first place.

candidates.name: JSON Schema Alignment Status Badge
candidates.pitch: Parses page network responses for JSON and validates against loaded schemas. Shows green/red badge in extension icon. Supports JSON Schema draft-07.
candidates.pain: Developers can't quickly tell if API responses on a page match their expected JSON schema.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Salesforce Task Deadline Countdown
candidates.pitch: Injects countdown timers on Salesforce task pages. Uses Salesforce Canvas or Chrome extension storage sync. Simple UI overlay makes it trivial to build.
candidates.pain: Sales reps miss task deadlines because they can't quickly see time remaining without opening Salesforce.

candidates.name: LinkedIn Connection Engagement Tracker
candidates.pitch: Tracks profile visits, reactions, and comments on connection content. Stores locally with optional sync. Built with LinkedIn's public data.
candidates.pain: Sales reps don't know which LinkedIn connections are most active, making outreach prioritization guesswork.

candidates.name: Gong Call Topic Drift Analyzer
candidates.pitch: Ingests Gong transcripts via API, runs basic NLP to detect topic changes. Visualizes as a timeline. Gong API access required.
candidates.pain: Sales managers can't quickly see if calls stayed on topic or drifted into off-agenda discussions.

candidates.name: PR Description Readability Calculator
candidates.pitch: Leverages Flesch-Kincaid readability scoring on PR descriptions. Integrates with GitHub PR UI. Could offer templates as premium tier.
candidates.pain: Pull request descriptions are often poorly written, making code reviews slower and less effective.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: CSS Custom Property Adoption Map
candidates.pitch: Scrapes all CSS custom properties from stylesheets and checks usage across page DOM. Visualizes adoption rate. Useful for design system audits.
candidates.pain: Design systems define CSS variables that developers rarely use, creating dead weight in the codebase.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)
